Photo by Daniele Bifolco

Traiano Arch

The Arch of Traiano in Benevento, a triumphal monument built in the 2nd century to honor Emperor Traiano.

Photo by Daniele Bifolco

Saint Sofia Square

Saint Sofia Square in Benevento, with its historic church, bell tower, and central obelisk fountain.

Photo by Daniele Bifolco

Roman Theater

The Roman Theater of Benevento, an ancient 2nd-century amphitheater still standing in the city’s historic center.

Photo by Daniele Bifolco

Bell tower of the Cathedral and the Sleeping Beauty mountain profile

Panoramic view featuring the bell tower of Benevento Cathedral and the “Sleeping Beauty” mountain profile on the horizon, bringing together the city’s architectural heritage and its distinctive surrounding landscape.

Photo by Daniele Bifolco

Skyline of Corso Garibaldi, Benevento

Panoramic view of Benevento’s historic city center, with Corso Garibaldi running through the urban landscape among traditional rooftops, historic buildings, and the rolling hills in the background.

ICSME 2026

Welcome to the website of the ICSME 2026 conference. We look forward to welcoming you to Benevento—the timeless host city of the 42nd International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution.

The Historic City of Benevento, Italy

Nestled in the heart of Southern Italy, Benevento is a city rich in history, culture, and tradition. Known since ancient times as the “City of Witches” (Città delle Streghe), it was once a strategic hub of the Roman Empire and later an important Lombard duchy. Today, it combines its fascinating past with a vibrant academic and cultural life.

Benevento’s historic centre is a treasure trove of UNESCO-recognized heritage, including the Arch of Trajan, the Church of Santa Sofia, and the Roman Theatre. The city is also home to the University of Sannio, fostering innovation, research, and international collaboration.

Surrounded by rolling hills and vineyards, Benevento is part of the celebrated Campania region, renowned for its gastronomy and wines, including the famous Falanghina and Aglianico. Visitors can stroll through medieval streets, enjoy traditional cuisine, and discover the unique folklore that makes Benevento one of Italy’s most distinctive destinations.

Discover the charm and character of this historic city in the video below!

More information about Benevento and its surroundings: beneventoturismo.it

Organizing Sponsors

Organizing Partners

With the patronage of

ICSME 2026 acknowledges the patronage of the University of Sannio, which provided our Venue.
